c74object.name: analyze~
c74object.digest: Performs spectral transience analysis on an audio signal
c74object.description:
The `analyze~` object performs real-time spectral analysis of an incoming audio signal. It utilizes an STFT-based Mel-spectrogram and Spectral Flux to detect transient peaks and calculate various transience-related metrics. The analysis is performed on a 1ms-hop basis and utilizes a background thread for expensive calculations to maintain audio stability.
This object uses a unified 15.2-second stateful analysis window, ensuring parity with offline Python-based analysis. Transients are detected across four frequency bands (Sub, Bass/Low-Mid, High-Mid, Treble) and assigned a "Resonance Score" based on their historical relationship with other peaks in a 5-second lookback buffer. The adaptive midpoint threshold for peak detection is calculated using a specialized dynamic historical peak-based sub-window.

inlet.description: Accepts the audio signal to be analyzed.
Accepts a signal representing the current transport clock time. This value is buffered and used to provide compensated timestamps for detected peaks.

outlet.description: Outputs a three-part list whenever a transient peak is detected. The first element is the frequency band index (0:Sub, 1:Bass, 2:Mid, 3:Hi), the second is the compensated transport clock value at the exact moment of the peak, and the third is the calculated transience score (Flux * Resonance Qualifier sum).
Outputs the absolute time offset (ms) of the highest peak in the current cumulative history buffer.
Outputs the average rating score calculated over the analysis history.
Outputs the current standard deviation of the analysis scores.
Outputs a score representing the spectral contrast detected in the signal.
Outputs the highest peak deviation currently observed in the analyzed signal.

attribute.name: group
attribute.description: Sets the name of the shared memory group. Objects with the same group name will share a cumulative history buffer, allowing their transience analysis to be context-aware of each other. This enables inter-stream rhythmic reinforcement and global transience density calculations. If no group is specified, the object uses a private buffer. Note: This attribute can only be set during object instantiation.

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: Enables or disables detailed diagnostic logging. When enabled (1), internal analysis states, peak detection events, and performance metrics are sent to the rightmost outlet. This is useful for debugging and fine-tuning the analyzer's behavior.
